steps.title: Read the intersection from the graph
steps.content: The solution to a graphed system is the point where the two lines cross. Here that point is at $x=2,\ y=4$.

steps.title: State the ordered pair
steps.content: The solution is $(2,4)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice B is correct. A solution to a system of equations must be the solution to each equation in the system. It follows that if $\left(x,y\right)$ is a solution to the system, the point $\left(x,y\right)$ lies on the graph in the xy-plane of each equation in the system. The point that lies on each graph of the system of linear equations shown is their intersection point $\left(2,4\right)$. Therefore, the solution to the system is $\left(2,4\right)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. The point $\left(0,5\right)$ lies on one, but not both, of the graphs of the linear equations shown.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. The point $\left(5,10\right)$ lies on one, but not both, of the graphs of the linear equations shown.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. The point $\left(10,0\right)$ lies on one, but not both, of the graphs of the linear equations shown.
